articles.title: MEMECOINS LEAD CRYPTO MARKET GAINS
articles.summary: Bitcoin remains range-bound below $70,000 while speculative assets drive market gains, with the AI tokens leading the sector. Derivatives markets show deep deleveraging with BTC futures open interest dropping to $15.9B and funding rates hitting aggressive negative territory on major exchanges (Binance -7%, Bybit -8%), though options data suggests institutional bottom-fishing with call dominance rebounding to 56%.

articles.title: GENIUS ACT AND MICA WILL SPLIT STABLECOINS
articles.summary: The GENIUS Act and MiCA are creating a bifurcated stablecoin market between Tier-1 "constitutional cash" tokens with strict redemption rights, liquid reserves, and no-yield provisions designed to prevent bank-like runs, and Tier-2 "synthetic cash" products that behave like money in calm markets but reprice as risk assets during redemption stress. MiCA reserves the authority to throttle stablecoins that achieve excessive payment ubiquity as a financial stability measure, while GENIUS explicitly bans yield to prevent digital dollars from mimicking uninsured deposits. The most critical unresolved vulnerability is cross-border "multi-issuance" structures where single stablecoin brands are issued by multiple legal entities across jurisdictions, creating concentration risk if global redemption runs overwhelm the jurisdiction with the strongest legal rights.

articles.title: LEDGER INTEGRATES OKX DEX FOR IN-WALLET, SELF-CUSTODIAL SWAPS
articles.summary: Ledger Wallet will integrate OKX DEX, allowing users to swap tokens directly onchain while retaining full self-custody and signing every transaction with a Ledger hardware device. The move expands Ledger's in-wallet DeFi stack with cross-chain swaps across major networks, pairing OKX's aggregated liquidity with hardware-enforced security to meet growing demand for autonomous yet secure trading.

articles.title: THE ZKEVM IS THE NEW 'MERGE'
articles.summary: zkEVM is Ethereum's new unifying narrative. It should replace the rollup-centric roadmap now that zkEVM technology has progressed faster than anticipated. The zkEVM would enable Ethereum's L1 to accept validity proofs of EVM execution rather than re-executing blocks, delivering an order-of-magnitude scalability improvement with low fees and fast transactions directly on mainnet. Unlike previous technical upgrades, this would directly address user experience by bringing massive scalability to L1 itself rather than pushing activity to L2s.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S NEAR-TERM ROLE IN AN AI-DRIVEN WORLD
articles.summary: Vitalik Buterin argues Ethereum's value in an AI-heavy future is not chasing AGI, but enabling privacy-preserving, decentralized coordination between humans and AI through trustless interaction, onchain economic mechanisms for agents, and AI-assisted governance and markets. He frames this as “defensive acceleration”, using tools like local LLMs, cryptographic payments, zero-knowledge proofs, and prediction markets to strengthen human agency and decentralized decision-making rather than concentrating power.

articles.title: THE AGENTIC ECOSYSTEM ON BASE
articles.summary: Base is positioned as the primary infrastructure layer for autonomous AI agents, leveraging x402 micropayments alongside deep USDC liquidity and Coinbase's infrastructure integration. The technical stack combines x402 for HTTP-native payments, CDP SDKs for wallet operations, XMTP for agent-to-agent messaging, and emerging identity frameworks to enable machine-to-machine commerce. There is structural fit rather than speculative appeal — autonomous systems require predictable execution costs and reliable capital access, making Base's low-fee environment and stablecoin infrastructure more important than high-upside tokenomics.

articles.title: ENSV2 TO DEPLOY ON ETHEREUM L1, NAMECHAIN CANCELLED
articles.summary: ENS announced ENSv2 will deploy exclusively on Ethereum L1, canceling plans for Namechain L2 while maintaining its product roadmap, including per-name registries and expanded customization. The protocol will enable one-click .eth registration from any EVM chain without bridging, alongside continued non-EVM support for Solana and Bitcoin. Vitalik Buterin has endorsed the decision, arguing that ENS represents central Ethereum ecosystem state functioning as a semi-financial application where L1 placement maximizes accessibility and aligns with an optimal decentralized identity architecture that keeps core account/profile data on mainnet while routing user actions to specialized L2s.

articles.title: EUROPE'S $24T BREAKUP WITH VISA AND MASTERCARD HAS BEGUN
articles.summary: The European Payments Initiative and EuroPA Alliance partnered to launch Wero, a digital payment network serving 130 million users across 13 countries. Utilizing SEPA instant transfers, the project seeks to establish European strategic autonomy and reduce reliance on US-based infrastructure like Visa and Mastercard.

articles.title: UNDERSTANDING TELEGRAM'S BUILT-IN TON WALLET
articles.summary: Telegram is expanding its built-in TON wallet to 87 million users in the US, integrating cryptocurrency management directly into the messaging platform.

articles.title: UNC1069 TARGETS FINANCIAL FIRMS WITH AI-DRIVEN ATTACKS
articles.summary: North Korean threat actor UNC1069 is targeting the cryptocurrency and DeFi sectors using AI-powered social engineering.

articles.title: BASE APP DROPS FARCASTER TALK FEED TO DOUBLE DOWN ON ONCHAIN TRADING
articles.summary: The Base App removed its Farcaster-powered Talk feed and is winding down the Base Creator Rewards program (which paid out more than $450,000 to ~17,000 creators) as it pivots to a trading-first wallet that surfaces only tradable onchain assets and activity.

articles.title: ERC-8128: SIGNED HTTP REQUESTS WITH ETHEREUM
articles.summary: A new signature-based authentication standard cryptographically binds Ethereum identity and intent to every HTTP request.
